title: Quiz: QAOA Applications
description: Test your understanding of practical QAOA applications

questions.question: In MaxCut, what does the cost function measure?

options.text: Total weight of edges crossing the partition

questions.explanation: MaxCut aims to maximize the total weight of edges that cross between the two partitions of vertices.

questions.question: In portfolio optimization with QAOA, what does the risk aversion parameter λ control?

options.text: Trade-off between expected return and portfolio variance

questions.explanation: The risk aversion parameter λ balances maximizing returns against minimizing portfolio variance (risk).

questions.question: What is 'makespan' in scheduling problems?

options.text: Total processing time of all jobs
Time when the last job completes

questions.explanation: Makespan is the completion time of the last job, i.e., the maximum load across all machines.

questions.question: In QAOA for clustering, points assigned to the same cluster should have high similarity scores.
questions.explanation: Good clustering groups similar points together, so the cost function rewards placing high-similarity pairs in the same cluster.

questions.question: How are constraints typically encoded in QAOA optimization problems?

options.text: By reducing the number of qubits
Using penalty terms in the cost Hamiltonian

questions.explanation: Constraints are added as penalty terms that increase the cost when violated, discouraging infeasible solutions.

questions.question: For job scheduling with n jobs and m machines, how many binary variables are typically needed?
questions.explanation: With one-hot encoding, variable x_{j,m}=1 indicates job j is assigned to machine m, requiring n×m binary variables.

questions.question: What is the approximation ratio in QAOA benchmarking?

options.text: QAOA solution cost divided by optimal cost

questions.explanation: The approximation ratio measures how close the QAOA solution is to optimal: QAOA_cost / optimal_cost.

questions.question: Which are valid applications of QAOA? (Select all)
questions.explanation: QAOA is designed for discrete combinatorial problems: graph partitioning (a), vehicle routing (c), and feature selection (d). Continuous optimization (b) requires different approaches.
